title: Working with Language Setups
text: Working with Language Setups Language setups allow you to deploy a Uniface application in different languages. Load source definitions for standard language setups from libmsg.usys.xml , located in the uniface\misc directory of your installation. Create a Library. Import the libmsg.usys.xml : /cpy d:Uniface\uniface\misc\libmsg.usys.xml Edit the language setups. If you want to define your own language setups, first create a new library or use one you already created for global objects. For more information, see Language Setups . Choose More Editors and then select the one of the language setups: Date-time properties —date and time formats. Editor messages —Uniface text editor messages Button Text —button text in panels, as well as texts for structure editor functions such as Next Occurrence Toggle Symbols —labels for the Character Attributes menu, and check boxes in panels Japanese Eras —Japanese eras based on the reigns of the emperors Select or create the library to contain the language definitions. For more information, see Create a Library for Modeled Properties or Global Objects . Select an existing definition or define a new one. For example, to create a French language definition, enter FRA as the Name and click OK . In the resulting dialog, set the properties of the type of language setup. For help on specific properties, press F1 . Apply a language definition by setting the $language ProcScript function or the $LANGUAGE assignment setting to the desired language. Setting $language in ProcScript overrides the $LANGUAGE assignment setting. Setting the application language affects not only the language setups, but also global objects such as menus, glyphs, messages, and help texts. Distribute a language setup: Export the language setup using the /lse command line switch. Import using the /cpy command line switch. /cpy /sup xml: FileName , where FileName is the name of the file containing the new language setup. Compile the language setups using the /mes command line switch or choose File > Compile . Related Links $LANGUAGE Specify the language to use for global objects and language setups. $language Return or set the current language code used for language-related global objects and language setups. /lse Copy language setups to an XML file. /cpy Copy one or more entity occurrences from a source database or XML file to another. /mes Compile global messages, help texts, and language setups.
